method: AR(1) with rho=0.3, sigma=0.01, 500 Monte Carlo replications, evaluate at t=4500 of a 5000-step series. NaN-safe rolling std (skips NaN within window) and z-score (uses past L values [t-L, t-1]).
headline: Paper's claim Var(beta_hat) ~ w^-1 is NOT supported by simulation. log-log slope in w = +0.204 (variance slightly grows in w). log-log slope in L = -0.169 (variance shrinks in L but much slower than L^-1). The four compositions + z-score + OLS-of-4-points structure mean the rate is dominated by something other than the simple w^-1 or L^-1 of a textbook estimator.
